case_name: Varela   v Buckswood School Ltd [2025] EWHC 2445 (KB) (26 September 2025)

evidence_index.text: The Claimant, Ms Irune Pedrayes Varela, brings a claim against Buckswood School Limited in relation to matters occurring in the autumn term of 2019, when the Claimant was 14 years of age and attending the school as a 'full-time boarder'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Irune Pedrayes alleges that over the weekend of 27 to 29 September 2019, and whilst on the school's premises, she was supplied by an older pupil with a drug which she was told was called 'magic' and which she took in a vape.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The nurse recorded that the Claimant told her that that she had taken 'some drugs yesterday in a vape at about 11am', that she 'has a headache', that 'she has black in her vision, feels that things are repeating themselves'; the nurse noted 'paranoid and manic symptoms obvious'.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The school arranged for the Claimant to be taken to the local hospital where she was seen by a doctor and assessed as suffering the effects of recreational drug use; she returned to school that afternoon.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Claimant alleges that the school informed her parents that she had been vaping and that she had had to go to hospital, but did not inform her parents that she had admitted to taking 'magic' or any drug
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: It is common ground between the psychiatrists that the Claimant's consumption of illicit drugs, starting with 'magic' whilst at the school, precipitated an episode of psychosis and mania
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.text: The Defendant, Buckswood School Limited, accepts that it owed a duty of care to the Claimant, but denies that it breached that duty
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Quantum was agreed in the sum of £145,000 (£60,000 for general damages and £85,000 for special damages), inclusive of interest.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In my judgment, the totality of the evidence does not support the conclusion that there was any systemic failure on the part of the school to take reasonable care to prevent access to drugs
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The Claimant's case on Issue 1 succeeds. In my judgment, the Defendant school acted negligently, in breach of its duty of care to the Claimant, in failing to inform the Claimant's parents timeously
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The Claimant's case on Issue 2 is dismissed for the reasons set out at [103] to [118] above
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The Claimant's case on Issue 3 is dismissed for the reasons set out at [120] to [146] above
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Accordingly, there will be judgment for the Claimant on Issue 1 with damages agreed in the sum of £60,000 for general damages and £85,000 for special damages, inclusive of interest.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

patient_demographics.relevant_social_history: Boarding student at Buckswood School, parents living in Spain

initial_state.chief_complaint: Feeling unwell with headache and visual disturbances
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient reports taking 'some drugs' in a vape the previous day, experiencing headache, blackness in vision, and feelings of repetition.
initial_state.initial_working_problem: Acute symptoms following suspected drug use

requestables.name: Urine Test
requestables.description: Urine test performed to check for drug use

reveal.clinical_significance: Indicates dehydration and possible substance use
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt further investigation and parental notification

requestables.name: Hospital Assessment
requestables.description: Assessment by doctor at local hospital

reveal.result_summary: Symptoms of distress, disorientation, visual disturbance, and paranoid thoughts
reveal.clinical_significance: Attributed to effects of recreational drug use
reveal.decision_impact: Should have led to informing parents about drug use

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to school nurse with symptoms

key_events.event: Patient reports drug use and symptoms to nurse

timeline_phases.name: Hospital Assessment
timeline_phases.description: Patient taken to hospital for further assessment

key_events.event: Assessment at hospital, symptoms attributed to drug use

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient presented with symptoms after drug use
decision_points.prompt: What information should be communicated to the patient's parents?

options.description: Inform parents about the drug use and symptoms
options.clinical_reasoning: Parents need to be aware to make informed decisions about care

options.description: Inform parents only about general unwellness
options.clinical_reasoning: Failed to provide full disclosure of drug use and symptoms

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant informed parents only about general unwellness
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: School did not appreciate the severity of the situation

expected_action_court.description: Parents should have been informed about drug use and symptoms
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Duty to inform parents of significant health issues

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to inform parents prevented them from taking necessary actions
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Parents should have been informed to ensure proper care
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care for boarding schools

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed psychiatric injuries requiring treatment
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Psychotic episode, PTSD, hallucinogen perception disorder

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to inform parents about drug use
Causation established - lack of information delayed appropriate intervention

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to inform parents about drug use and symptoms
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Timely information could have led to earlier intervention

factual_timeline.event: Patient reports drug use and symptoms to school nurse
Patient assessed at hospital for drug-related symptoms

treatments.treatment: Hospital assessment and advice to drink fluids

allegations.allegation: Failure to inform parents about drug use

court_findings.legal_significance: Failure to inform parents of significant health issues
